Pass Criteria: All required collections exist with exact names.
Every nucleus object is parented to Nucleus_RootRig or a child collection under it.
Outer radius about 3.2 BU and fits inside whole cell context.
Nucleus sits inside cytoplasm at NucleusSocket_Center, not floating separately.
Nucleus_RootRig origin is centered and rotations do not offset shell.
Membrane panels split outward cleanly during mitosis.
Outer membrane, inner membrane, and perinuclear space are all visible.
NPCs read as protein ring complexes with gate cores, not simple holes.
Chromatin/chromosomes never animate outside the nucleus.
RNA exports outward and protein cargo imports inward through NPCs.
Nucleolus is dense and bumpy but has no membrane shell.
Lamina lattice lies just under the inner membrane.
Outer nuclear membrane visibly connects to rough ER segments.
Model has depth, texture, bevels, shadows, and readable material separation.
Interior can be seen from main camera without losing shell identity.
Materials match the color system and are consistent across parts.
Membranes, nucleolus, chromatin, and lamina have distinct procedural texture/bump.
Subtle drift/pulse animation exists frames 1-120.
Green cargo moves from cytoplasm into nucleus through NPC frames 130-210.
Pink RNA/ribosomal cargo moves out through NPC frames 220-300.
Chromatin hotspot glows and generates RNA frames 310-390.
Chromatin condenses, nucleolus fades, lamina fragments, envelope panels separate frames 400-560.
Envelope reforms, NPCs reinsert, lamina returns, nucleolus reappears frames 570-720.
Functional components remain separate for scripting/animation.
Nucleus LOD0 target under 90k triangles or clearly flagged if higher.
Panels/sheets have thickness and avoid single-sided disappearing faces.
Materials can be approximated in Roblox with base color, alpha, roughness, emission.
Optional labels exist in 99_REVIEW_CHECKPOINTS and can be hidden.
Main, NPC close-up, and component/exploded cameras exist.
A learner can identify envelope, pores, lamina, chromatin, nucleolus, transport, and ER connection.
